title: Neobank settles running accounts annually, email-only intimation
scenario: We are launching a retail broking app. We settle client running accounts once a year. After settlement we notify clients by email only. When our operations team is busy we retain client funds to ease processing.
notes: annual settlement violates monthly/quarterly choice (001); email-only misses required SMS (012); retaining funds for ops difficulties is barred (009)

title: Journal-entry settlement, idle balances not returned
scenario: Our back office settles client running accounts by passing journal entries rather than transferring money to the client bank account. Clients with a credit balance who have stopped trading keep their balance with us until the next quarterly cycle.
notes: settlement must be actual bank payment not journal entry (006); idle credit balances must be returned within 3 working days of 30-day inactivity (007)

title: Authorised persons handling client money
scenario: Our authorised persons collect funds and securities directly from clients to speed onboarding, and they run their own proprietary trades using the pooled client money when markets are active.
notes: AP must not accept client funds/securities (010); AP prop trading only on own funds (011)

title: Bank guarantees from client funds, no EOD upstreaming
scenario: To increase our trading limits we create bank guarantees backed by the pooled client funds. Client clear credit balances stay in our house account and are swept to the clearing corporation weekly.
notes: no new BGs from client funds (BGCF-001); clear credit balances must be upstreamed to CCs on EOD basis, not weekly (UPSTREAM-001)

title: Unpaid client securities pledged to a bank
scenario: When clients do not pay in full, we move the unpaid securities into a pledgee account and pledge them to our bank to raise working capital. We hold the pledge open indefinitely until the client pays.
notes: unpaid client securities must not be pledged to banks/NBFCs for funds (CLTSEC-011); pledge auto-releases if not invoked/released within 7 trading days (CLTSEC-009)

title: Compliant running-account process (no-gap control)
scenario: We settle client running accounts monthly or quarterly per each client's documented choice on the exchange-stipulated dates, send both SMS and email intimation with transfer details on settlement, return idle credit balances to inactive clients within three working days, and upstream all clear credit balances to the clearing corporation at end of day.
declared_controls: monthly/quarterly settlement per client choice on exchange dates
SMS and email intimation with transfer details
return idle credit balances within 3 working days
EOD upstreaming of client credit balances
notes: control case: a compliant scenario must yield NO findings (guards against hallucinated gaps)
